key_insight: The best-performing alloys sit right on the edge of phase stability, so the paper’s main message is that risk-aware Bayesian optimization can find high-strength compositions that a hard-feasibility filter would miss.
engineering_relevance: For alloy design, this shows how to simultaneously optimize performance and experimental feasibility, enabling faster discovery of candidate compositions with practical synthesis success.
method: Built a Bayesian optimization loop with a learned feasibility classifier embedded in the acquisition function.
Screened ~27,000 CALPHAD candidates and synthesized 48 alloys over 3 closed-loop iterations.
Optimized five targets at once: strength, ductility, hardness-rate sensitivity, and simulated penetration performance.
evidence_signal: The campaign explored only 0.12% of the feasible space yet found alloys up to ~1480 MPa UTS at 50% elongation and identified a narrow high-performing region around V ≥ 20 at.% and Ni ≥ 36 at.%.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality of the CALPHAD screening and feasibility classifier, so model bias or misclassification could still exclude promising chemistries or admit failed experiments.
paper_title: A BRAVE Alloy Design Campaign (Bayesian Risk-aware Alloy discoVery and Exploration)
